description_long: This object is a tall, slender obelisk with a rectangular cross-section and a flat top. The main shaft is covered with a dark, weathered plaque or surface that features a dense block of inscribed text, possibly commemorative or informational, which appears to be in a sans-serif font and is somewhat faded. The obelisk is framed by a lighter, smooth border that contrasts with the central dark panel. The sides are extremely thin, emphasizing the monolithic and vertical nature of the structure. The base is simple and rectangular, with no visible steps or elaborate features, and the overall design is utilitarian rather than ornate. The object appears to be made primarily of stone or concrete, with the central plaque possibly being metal or a different textured material.
description: A tall, rectangular obelisk with a dark inscribed plaque framed by a lighter border and a simple, flat base. The design is minimalistic and functional, with dense text on the main face.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full inscribed plaque with dense text and the light border framing the dark central panel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the extreme thinness of the obelisk and the flat, unadorned edge.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the same inscribed plaque and border.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and flat edge.
attribute_inscription: A large, dense block of text is inscribed on the central dark plaque on both the front and rear faces. The text is in a sans-serif font, appears to be in English, and is somewhat faded, making it partially illegible.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, flat, rectangular extension of the obelisk, with no steps, platforms, or decorative features. It is made of the same light-colored material as the border.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and unadorned, matching the rectangular cross-section of the shaft with no unique features or decorations.
